item.title: 使用 Laravel 9x 构建 REST API | Building REST API with Laravel 9x
item.description:
出色的控制容器反转、富有表现力的迁移系统和紧密集成的单元测试支持为您提供了构建您负责的任何应用程序所需的工具。

但是，我们将学习如何使用 Laravel 9x 构建 REST API 的步骤。

创建你的 Laravel 项目

在创建你的 laravel 项目之前，你需要确保 PHP 和Composer安装在你的本地计算机上，以便执行artisan命令。 如果您在同一个文件上构建前端，我建议您为 NPM 命令安装Node 。 但是对于这个内容，我们将使用 Postman 进行测试，我建议您阅读我的内容，了解如何使用 laravel 设置 postman 。

要创建 laravel 项目，请运行以下命令

composer global require laravel/installer laravel new laravel-9x

第一个命令composer global require laravel/installer用于在本地机器上安装laravel 。

注意： laravel-9x可以替换为您选择的任何首选名称，因为它用作项目名称。

创建项目后， cd进入项目目录。

使用上述命令迁移到您的项目后，运行下面的下一个命令。

上面的命令在我们的项目上创建了一个新的vendor文件夹，并允许访问 run 以运行artisan命令。

设置你的数据库

要在 laravel 上设置数据库，请在项目文件中找到 .env 文件。

 DB_CONNECTION=mysql DB_HOST=127.0.0.1 DB_PORT=3306 DB_DATABASE=testproject DB_USERNAME=root DB_PASSWORD=

对于这个项目，我将使用MySQL数据库，同时我有上面的代码片段。

如上所示创建数据库连接后，您可以运行以下命令来迁移数据库模型。

 php artisan key:generate php artisan migrate

上面的命令为您的项目在.env文件上生成一个APP_KEY ，而第二个命令migrate您的数据库。

创建数据库迁移

迁移文件通常是根据您的数据库表的首选名称生成的。

我们将生成一个名为contact的迁移文件，它将用作测试我们的REST API的联系人表。

 php artisan make:model Contact -m

上面的命令会生成一个model和数据库迁移文件，叫做contact 。

在database/migrations/2022_09_16_042805_create_contacts_table.php上找到项目内的迁移。 请注意，文件名是用附加的当前数据命名的。

 public function up() { Schema::create('contacts', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->string('name'); $table->string('email'); $table->string('tel'); $table->timestamps(); }); }

复制up()函数并将其替换为上面的代码片段。

注意，我们只是将下面的代码块添加到函数up()

 $table->string('name'); $table->string('email'); $table->string('tel');

完成上述操作后，运行迁移以在数据库上migrate/create表contact 。

创建您的 API 路由
在 laravel 中，我们可以在routes/api.php文件中注册我们所有的 API 路由。

我们将在middleware路由功能之前注册两个post API 路由。 我们正在创建save_data和fetch_data路由。

 Route::post('save_data', [ContactController::class, 'save_data'])->name('api.save_data'); Route::post('fetch_data', [ContactController::class, 'fetch_data'])->name('api.fetch_data');

上面的代码块演示了您的路线应该如何锁定。

 <?php use Illuminate\Http\Request; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route; use App\Http\Controllers\ContactController; Route::post('save_data', [ContactController::class, 'save_data'])->name('api.save_data'); Route::post('fetch_data', [ContactController::class, 'fetch_data'])->name('api.fetch_data'); Route::middleware('auth:sanctum')->get('/user', function (Request $request) { return $request->user(); });

这是我的整个api.php路由文件代码，你可以复制并替换为你的。

创建控制器

控制器可以将相关的请求处理逻辑分组到一个类中。
控制器是控制请求行为的控制器。 它处理来自路由的请求。 在 Laravel 中，控制器位于app/Http/Controllers目录中。

我们将使用以下命令创建一个名为 ContactController 的控制器。

 php artisan make:controller ContactController

创建控制器后，我们可以在app/Http/Controllers上找到控制器。

写下你的代码

这是本文最甜蜜的部分是编码方面，这里可以根据你的编码偏好编写你的代码，但为了更清楚，你可以参考我的代码。

在ContactController.php中，根据routes/api.php上的路由，我们将拥有两个函数，其中我们有save_data和fetch_data路由。

 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use Illuminate\Http\Request; class ContactController extends Controller { // code here }

上面的代码块代表了我们在里面编写函数之前的ContactController.php中的所有内容。

现在，我们将编写我们的第一个函数，即save_data函数。

 public function save_data(request $request) { if(isset($request->name) && isset($request->email) && isset($request->tel)){ // proccess data $contact = new contact; $contact->email = $request->email; $contact->name = $request->name; $contact->tel = $request->tel; if ($contact->save()) { return response()->json([ 'status' => 'success', 'message' => 'Data saved successfully', 'data' => $contact ]); } else { return response()->json([ 'status' => 'error', 'message' => 'An error occured' ],500); } }else{ return response()->json([ 'status' => 'error', 'message' => 'Missing Paramiter' ],501); } }

如果变量存在，此函数返回 true。

上面的代码块会将来自我们 REST API 路由的数据保存到数据库中的contact模型中。

对于本文，我将使用postman进行测试，如果您是 postman 新手，我建议您查看这篇关于如何使用 laravel 设置 postman 的文章。

从邮递员那里，我们将根据save_data函数的要求发送name 、 email和tel 。

如果您在上面的快照中发送了与我一样的确切数据，您将得到与我相同的响应。

 "status": "success", "message": "Data saved successfully", "data": { "email": "okechinoble@gmail.com", "name": "Noble Okechi", "tel": "+2349097784784", "updated_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z", "created_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z", "id": 1 }

现在，我们已经实现了save_data函数，是时候构建我们​​的 fetch_data 函数来从contact模型中获取所有存储的数据了。

 public function fetch_data() { $fetch = contact::latest()->get(); if ($fetch) { return response()->json([ 'status' => 'success', 'message' => 'Data fetched successfully', 'data' => $fetch ]); } else { return response()->json([ 'status' => 'error', 'message' => 'An error occured' ],500); } }

它使用created_at列对其数据进行排序。

现在，我们可以在 postman 上创建fetch_data请求来测试fetch_data功能。

在设置邮递员请求以从 fetch_data API 获取数据后，我得到了一个数据数组作为下面的响应。

 "status": "success", "message": "Data fetched successfully", "data": [ { "id": 2, "name": "Developer Noble", "email": "dev@gmail.com", "tel": "+234000000000", "created_at": "2022-09-16T15:20:39.000000Z", "updated_at": "2022-09-16T15:20:39.000000Z" }, { "id": 1, "name": "Noble Okechi", "email": "okechinoble@gmail.com", "tel": "+2349097784784", "created_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z", "updated_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z" } ]

哇，如果你已经达到了这个程度，布拉沃斯。

代码模块

我们现在可以通过逐个模块发布整个代码来包装我们的代码。

接触控制器模块

联系人迁移模块

如果您还记得，我们在迁移文件夹中建立了我们的数据库联系人模型字段，并且可以位于database/migrations 。

 <?php use Illuminate\Database\Migrations\Migration; use Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Schema; return new class extends Migration { /** * Run the migrations. * * @return void */ public function up() { Schema::create('contacts', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->string('name'); $table->string('email'); $table->string('tel'); $table->timestamps(); }); } /** * Reverse the migrations. * * @return void */ public function down() { Schema::dropIfExists('contacts'); } };

结论

在本文中，我们成功探索了 laravel 9x 中的不同模块，并演示了如何在 laravel 9x 上构建 REST API 的简单步骤。

使用 laravel 9x，很容易为每个前端构建标准的 REST API。



原文:

Laravel is a PHP framework that is build for web Artisan. Laravel attempts to take the pain out of development by easing common tasks used in the majority of web projects, such as authentication, routing, sessions, and caching.

Laravel aims to make the development process a pleasing one for the developer without sacrificing application functionality.

Laravel is accessible, yet powerful, providing powerful tools needed for large, robust applications. A superb inversion of control container, expressive migration system, and tightly integrated unit testing support give you the tools you need to build any application with which you are tasked.

Laravel is a backend framework that provides all of the features you need to build modern web applications, such as routing, validation, caching, queues, file storage, and more. However, we will be learning steps on how to build REST API with Laravel 9x.

Create your Laravel Project

Before creating your laravel project, you need to ensure that PHP and Composer is installed on your local computer for artisan commands. If you are building frontend on a same file, I recommend you install Node for NPM commands. But for this content, we shall be using Postman for testing and I recommend you to go through my content on how to setup postman with laravel.

To create a laravel project, run the commands below

composer global require laravel/installer

laravel new laravel-9x

composer is a dependency manager for PHP that provides a standard format for managing dependencies of PHP software and required libraries.

The first command composer global require laravel/installer is used to install laravel on your local machine.

laravel new laravel-9x command installs a fresh laravel project on your computer.

Note: laravel-9x can be replace with any preferred name of your choice, as it serves as the project name.

After the project has been created, cd into the project directory.

After migrating into your project with the above command, run the next command below.

The above command creates a new vendor folder on our project and gives access to run to run artisan commands.

Setup your Database

To setup your database on laravel, locate .env file inside your project files.

DB_CONNECTION=mysql
DB_HOST=127.0.0.1
DB_PORT=3306
DB_DATABASE=testproject
DB_USERNAME=root
DB_PASSWORD=

For this project, I will be using MySQL database and that is while I have the above code snippet.

DB_DATABASE=testproject represents your database file name, which testproject can be changed to any preferred name of your choice.

Having created a database connection as shown above, you can run the below command to migrate your database model.

php artisan key:generate

php artisan migrate

The command above generate an APP_KEY on the .env file for your project, while the second command migrate your database.

Create a Database Migration

A migration file in laravel represents a database table file in the core PHP code. Migration file is often generated according preferred name of your database table.

We will generate a migration file called contact, which will serve as contact table for testing our REST API.

php artisan make:model Contact -m

The above command will generate a model and database migration file called contact.

Locate the migration inside your project on database/migrations/2022_09_16_042805_create_contacts_table.php. Note that the file name is been named with the current data attached to it.

public function up()
    {
        Schema::create('contacts', function (Blueprint $table) {
            $table->id();
            $table->string('name');
            $table->string('email');
            $table->string('tel');
            $table->timestamps();
        });
    }

Copy and replace the up() function with the snippet above.

Note, we only added the code block below to the function up()

$table->string('name');
$table->string('email');
$table->string('tel');

After completing the above, run migration to migrate/create your table contact on your database.

Create your API routes
In laravel, we can register all our API routes inside routes/api.php file.

We will be registering two post API routes before the middleware routes function. We are creating save_data and fetch_data routes.

Route::post('save_data', [ContactController::class, 'save_data'])->name('api.save_data');
Route::post('fetch_data', [ContactController::class, 'fetch_data'])->name('api.fetch_data');

The above code block demonstrate how your route should lock like.

<?php

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route;
use App\Http\Controllers\ContactController;


Route::post('save_data', [ContactController::class, 'save_data'])->name('api.save_data');
Route::post('fetch_data', [ContactController::class, 'fetch_data'])->name('api.fetch_data');

Route::middleware('auth:sanctum')->get('/user', function (Request $request) {
    return $request->user();
});

This is my entire api.php route file codes, you can copy and replace with yours.

Create a Controller

Controllers can group related request handling logic into a single class.
A Controller is that which controls the behavior of a request. It handles the requests coming from the Routes. In Laravel, a controller is in the app/Http/Controllers directory.

We will have create a controller named ContactController using the command below.

php artisan make:controller ContactController

After creating our controller, we can locate the controller on app/Http/Controllers.

Write your codes

This is the sweetest part of this article is the coding aspect, here can write your codes according your coding preference, but for a better clarity, you can make reference to my codes.

Inside the ContactController.php, we will be having two functions according to our routes on routes/api.php, where we have save_data and fetch_data routes.

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class ContactController extends Controller
{
    // code here
}

The above code block, represents everything in our ContactController.php before we write our functions inside.

Now, we will be writing our first function, which is the save_data function.

 public function save_data(request $request)
    {
        if(isset($request->name) && isset($request->email) && isset($request->tel)){
            // proccess data
            $contact = new contact;
            $contact->email = $request->email;
            $contact->name = $request->name;
            $contact->tel = $request->tel;
            if ($contact->save()) {
                return response()->json([
                    'status' => 'success',
                    'message' => 'Data saved successfully',
                    'data' => $contact
                ]);
            } else {
                return response()->json([
                    'status' => 'error',
                    'message' => 'An error occured'
                ],500);
            }

        }else{
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'error',
                'message' => 'Missing Paramiter'
            ],501);
        }
    }

The isset() function checks whether a variable is set, which means that it has to be declared and is not NULL. This function returns true if the variable exists.

The above code block will save data coming from our REST API routes into our contact model in the database.

For this article, I will be testing with postman, if you are new to postman, I will suggest you check this article on how to setup postman with laravel.

From postman, we will be sending name, email, and tel as required on the save_data function.

If you have sent the exact data as mine on the snapshot above, you will be getting same response as mine.

"status": "success",
    "message": "Data saved successfully",
    "data": {
        "email": "okechinoble@gmail.com",
        "name": "Noble Okechi",
        "tel": "+2349097784784",
        "updated_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z",
        "created_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z",
        "id": 1
    }

Now, that we have achieved the save_data function, it is time to work build our fetch_data function for getting all stored data from contact model.

public function fetch_data()
    {
        $fetch = contact::latest()->get();
        if ($fetch) {
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'success',
                'message' => 'Data fetched successfully',
                'data' => $fetch
            ]);
        } else {
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'error',
                'message' => 'An error occured'
            ],500);
        }

    }

latest() fetches the most recent set of data from the Database. it sort its data using the created_at column.

Now, we can create fetch_data request on postman to test the fetch_data function.

After setting up postman request to fetch data from fetch_data API, I got an array of data as response below.

"status": "success",
    "message": "Data fetched successfully",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 2,
            "name": "Developer Noble",
            "email": "dev@gmail.com",
            "tel": "+234000000000",
            "created_at": "2022-09-16T15:20:39.000000Z",
            "updated_at": "2022-09-16T15:20:39.000000Z"
        },
        {
            "id": 1,
            "name": "Noble Okechi",
            "email": "okechinoble@gmail.com",
            "tel": "+2349097784784",
            "created_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z",
            "updated_at": "2022-09-16T14:47:45.000000Z"
        }
    ]

Wow, if you have gotten it to this extent, bravos.

Code Modules

We can now wrap up our code by posting the entire codes module by module.

ContactController Module

ContactController module is located at app/Http/Controllers

<?php

namespace App\Http\Controllers;

use App\Models\contact;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;

class ContactController extends Controller
{
    public function save_data(request $request)
    {
        if(isset($request->name) && isset($request->email) && isset($request->tel)){
            // proccess data
            $contact = new contact;
            $contact->email = $request->email;
            $contact->name = $request->name;
            $contact->tel = $request->tel;
            if ($contact->save()) {
                return response()->json([
                    'status' => 'success',
                    'message' => 'Data saved successfully',
                    'data' => $contact
                ]);
            } else {
                return response()->json([
                    'status' => 'error',
                    'message' => 'An error occured'
                ],500);
            }

        }else{
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'error',
                'message' => 'Missing Paramiter'
            ],501);
        }
    }

    public function fetch_data()
    {
        $fetch = contact::latest()->get();
        if ($fetch) {
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'success',
                'message' => 'Data fetched successfully',
                'data' => $fetch
            ]);
        } else {
            return response()->json([
                'status' => 'error',
                'message' => 'An error occured'
            ],500);
        }

    }
}

Contact Migration Module

If you can recall, we buildup our database contact model fields inside migration folder and can be located at database/migrations.

<?php

use Illuminate\Database\Migrations\Migration;
use Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Schema;

return new class extends Migration
{
    /**
     * Run the migrations.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function up()
    {
        Schema::create('contacts', function (Blueprint $table) {
            $table->id();
            $table->string('name');
            $table->string('email');
            $table->string('tel');
            $table->timestamps();
        });
    }

    /**
     * Reverse the migrations.
     *
     * @return void
     */
    public function down()
    {
        Schema::dropIfExists('contacts');
    }
};

API Routes Modules

API routes is located at routes/api.php.

<?php

use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route;
use App\Http\Controllers\ContactController;


Route::post('save_data', [ContactController::class, 'save_data'])->name('api.save_data');
Route::post('fetch_data', [ContactController::class, 'fetch_data'])->name('api.fetch_data');

Route::middleware('auth:sanctum')->get('/user', function (Request $request) {
    return $request->user();
});

Conclusion

In this article, we successfully explored different modules in laravel 9x and we demonstrated easy steps on how to build REST APIs on laravel 9x.

With laravel 9x, it is easy to build standard REST APIs for every frontend.
item.author: nobleokechi

item.title: PagerDuty 社区每周更新，2022 年 9 月 16 日 | PagerDuty Community Weekly Update, September 16, 2022
item.description:
大家好！ 欢迎来到 PagerDuty 社区每周更新！ 在这里，您会发现 PagerDuty 土地上正在发生的事情。 这里忙碌的一周！

很酷的东西

对您个人而言，devops 历史上最具影响力的事件是什么

对您个人而言，DevOps 历史上影响最大的事件是什么？ 一些有趣的回复！ 你怎么看？ 让我们在评论中知道。

亲自找到我们！

我们即将举行现场面对面的会议！
也许您听说过 SLI、SLO、错误预算和混沌工程，但它们如何能够帮助您帮助您的用户。 快来看看@lnxchk和@julie_Gund 将向您展示如何将这些概念应用于您的项目并取悦您的用户
我们希望见到你！ 打个招呼，我们可能有 PagerDuty 贴纸。 我们的自动化人员正在寻求与 Rundeck 开源社区的成员进行一些面对面的会议。 如果是你，请告诉我们！

我们也是虚拟的！

现在可按需提供！

塔拉将展示故事使意义。 意义在Drupal 多元化与包容营中带来改变。 加入 Tara，深入了解讲故事及其如何在工作中为您提供帮助。 是的，即使你是工程师。

注册观看他的课程 服务水平目标：服务可靠性和高绩效团队背后的秘诀。 在我们的播客节目中聆听 Rich 的更多信息。

查看Conf42 网站上的活动详情。

在播客上

上周，PagerDuty 自己的 Jake Cohen 与 Mandi 一起谈论Runbook Automation 。 不要错过！ 在您最喜欢的播客应用上订阅 Page it to the Limit，或在线收听！

我们大约每月发布两次新剧集，因此请务必订阅，以免错过任何剧集。 接下来是服务网格！

在抽搐

我们每周在Twitch上 3 次，涵盖 PagerDuty 的所有内容。 我们的常规节目是： PagerDuty Garage，关于 PagerDuty API 的节目：星期一东部时间上午 10 点/太平洋时间上午 7 点 Terraform 时间：星期一东部时间下午 4 点/太平洋时间下午 1 点 HowTo 欢乐时光：星期五东部时间下午 4 点/太平洋时间下午 1 点。

当我们上线时，请关注我们的频道以获取警报！ 您还可以在Twitch上查看我们最近的剧集。 您可以在YouTube 播放列表中看到我们早期的一些流媒体。

流程自动化人员于 9 月 13 日回来与我们讨论 4.6.0版本！ 它很快就会出现在 YouTube 上。

注册提醒！

在博客上

您还可以在Dreamforce和Datadog DASH找到我们！

保持联系

如果您对 PagerDuty 有任何疑问，请查看我们的社区论坛以获取友好人士的建议。 如果您想加入我们的播客或 Twitch，或者只是打个招呼，请发送电子邮件至community-team@pagerduty.com 。 我们很想听听您使用 PagerDuty 做了什么！

在推特上关注我们！

  • 斯科特
  • 吉
  • 塔拉
  • 曼迪
  • 播客帐户
  • 一旦暂停，pdcommunity 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，pdcommunity 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦取消发布，pdcommunity 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 pdcommunity 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区👩‍💻👨‍💻 的安全。 以下是标记 pdcommunity 的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    取消标记 pdcommunity 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Hi everyone! Welcome to the PagerDuty Community Weekly Update! Here you’ll find what’s going on in PagerDuty land. Busy week here!

    Cool Stuff

    PagerDuty alum Matty Stratton asked on Twitter:

    For you personally, what was the most impactful event in the history of devops
    For you personally, what was the most impactful event in the history of DevOps? Some interesting replies! What do you think? Let us know in the comments.

    Find us in person!

    We have live in-person sessions coming up!
    Mandi is at DevOpsDays DC to co-present Reducing Trauma in Production with SLOs and Chaos Engineering with Julie Gunderson.

    Maybe you've heard about SLIs, SLOs, error budgets, and chaos engineering, but how can they can all help you help your users. Come see @lnxchk and @julie_Gund will show you how you can apply these concepts to your projects and delight your users
    #devops
    devopsdays.org/events/2022-wa…

    Kat will be at Dreamforce (nice!),September 19-22 in San Francisco, to present Automate Incident Response Between Operations & Customers, and at ElevateCX September 29 & 30 in Denver, to give her talk Happy Agents = Happy Customers.

    Scott is taking his talk Built-in Testing in Go is More Than Just Passable to GoLab.io October 2-4 in Florence, Italy and at Indy.Code() October 19!

    We hope to see you! Say hi, we might have PagerDuty stickers. :D

    Our automation folks are looking to start some in-person meetings with members of the Rundeck Open Source community. If that’s you, let us know!

    We’re also virtual!

    Mandi was part of the Community of Practice Roundtable session at ChefConf on Tuesday. Now available on demand!

    Kat was at the DeveloperWeek Cloud virtual followup this week to give her talk Happy Agents = Happy Customers.

    Tara will be presenting Stories Make Meaning. Meaning Makes Change at Drupal Diversity & Inclusion Camp. Join Tara for a deep dive into storytelling & how it can help you at work. Yes, even if you're an engineer.

    PagerDuty Staff SRE Rich Lafferty was part of Sumo Logic Illuminate September 13-14! Register to watch his session Service Level Objectives: The secret sauce behind service reliability and high performing teams. Hear more from Rich in his episode of our podcast.

    Mandi’s also going to be at Conf42 Incident Management on September 29th. Check out the event details on the Conf42 site.

    On the Podcast

    Last week, PagerDuty’s own Jake Cohen joined Mandi to talk about Runbook Automation. Don’t miss it! Subscribe to Page it to the Limit on your favorite podcast app, or listen online!

    We publish new episodes about twice a month, so make sure to subscribe so you don’t miss any. Next up is Service Mesh!

    On Twitch

    We’re on Twitch three times a week, covering all things PagerDuty. Our regular shows are: PagerDuty Garage, the show about the PagerDuty API: Mondays at 10am Eastern / 7am Pacific Terraform Time: Mondays at 4pm Eastern / 1pm Pacific HowTo Happy Hour: Fridays at 4pm Eastern / 1pm Pacific.

    Follow our channel for alerts when we go live! You can also view our most recent episodes on Twitch. You can see some of our earlier streams on the YouTube playlist.

    The Process Automation folks were back with us on September 13th to talk about the 4.6.0 release! It will be up on YouTube soon.

    Coming up later in September, our data science folks will be joining the HowTo Happy Hour to talk about some of the features of Intelligent Alert Grouping. Sign up for a reminder!

    On the Blog

    Salesforce and Datadog are important integrations for many PagerDuty users. Learn more about how these integrations work and what they can do for your teams! You can also find us at Dreamforce and Datadog DASH!

    Get in Touch

    If you have a question about PagerDuty, check out our Community Forums for advice from friendly folks. If you’d like to be on our podcast or Twitch, or just say hi, email us at community-team@pagerduty.com. We’d love to hear what you’re doing with PagerDuty!

    Follow us on Twitter!

    • Scott
    • Kat
    • Tara
    • Mandi
    • Podcast Account
    • PagerDuty Main

     🌚 Enable dark mode🔠 Change your default font

    📚 Adjust your experience level to see more relevant content

    Once suspended, pdcommunity will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, pdcommunity will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by pdcommunity will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If pdcommunity is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

    Thanks for keeping DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻 safe. Here is what you can do to flag pdcommunity:

    pdcommunity consistently posts content that violates DEV Community 👩‍💻👨‍💻's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

    Report other inappropriate conduct

    Unflagging pdcommunity will restore default visibility to their posts.
